customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety signals present. Nick is cooperative and curious — his unsolicited price question at call's end indicates genuine interest. Primary risk is structural (fragile booking) rather than customer-side resistance.
unresolved_concerns: Co-owner status unknown — Bucky confirmed title is in Nick's name but never asked if anyone else is on the title. Closer must establish this before proceeding.

No utility bill on file and no usage history — Nick just moved in last week, has not received a first bill, and has no established consumption baseline. Closer should plan to work from builder energy estimates or defer to first bill.

Credit score flagged by Nick as potentially dropping — he referenced a likely hard inquiry from the new home purchase. Stated 800 but volunteered it 'might be down a little.' Closer should confirm via pull rather than relying on stated number.

Lead type not confirmed in transcript — unclear whether this is a paid lead (bill required before sit) or organic (bill preferred). If paid, the absence of a bill on file is a gating issue the closer needs to address.
sensitive_topics_raised: New construction home — not yet on Google Maps, moved in last week. Closer should not assume standard satellite imagery is available for roof design.
exact_promises_rep_made: Text reminder sent after the call
closer_should_know: Nick is a brand-new homeowner (moved in last week, new construction) with no utility bill yet and no usage history — the design will need to be built from scratch. He asked about price unprompted before hanging up, which is a genuine curiosity signal. The co-owner question was never asked; establish that immediately. Do not assume the satellite roof image is current — the house is not yet on Google Maps.
